destinations.evidence: The registry helper returns true only for nonzero executor jobs whose expiryBlock is strictly greater than the current block.
Payload building consults the active-job helper and skips only duplicate commitments whose existing job is still active.
Block verification mirrors the builder and rejects only active duplicate async jobs.

equivalence_argument: Builder and verifier both delegate duplicate detection to job_exists_and_active, whose nonzero-executor plus expiryBlock > current_block_number predicate allows missing and expired jobs while rejecting active duplicates.
notes: The expiry boundary is shared through the registry helper, so builder/verifier parity follows the same storage reads.
rationale: The merge adds `job_exists_and_active`, uses it in both payload builder and verifier, and explicitly treats expired jobs as non-existent for duplicate-commitment purposes while preserving rejection of active jobs. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Payload building and block verification should reject a new async commitment when `jobExecutor[txHash]` is nonzero and `expiryBlock[txHash] > current_block_number`, but should allow the commitment when the job is missing or expired with `expiryBlock <= current_block_number`.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set up AsyncJobTracker storage with the same job id under four cases: no executor, nonzero executor with expiry greater than current block, nonzero executor with expiry equal to current block, and nonzero executor with expiry below current block. Assert builder/verifier reject only the active case and allow the missing or expired cases.
summary: Builder and verifier reject only active duplicate async commitments.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set AsyncJobTracker storage for missing, active, expiry equal, and expired jobs and assert builder/verifier reject only the active duplicate.
title: Async commitments are rejected only for active duplicate jobs
